relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus reveals himself again by the sea of Tiberias.
The disciples gather: Peter, Thomas, Nathanael, Zebedee’s sons, and two others.
They fish through the night and catch nothing.
At daybreak, Jesus stands on shore unrecognized.
He calls them children and asks about fish.
The right-side cast becomes abundance too heavy to haul.
The beloved disciple recognizes the Lord; Peter throws himself toward him.
The others drag the full net to shore.
A charcoal fire waits with fish and bread.
Jesus invites their catch into the meal.
Peter draws the full, untorn net of one hundred fifty-three fish.
Come, have breakfast; knowing replaces questioning.
Jesus gives bread and fish.
This is the third revelation after resurrection.
The first love-question becomes feed my lambs.
The second love-question becomes shepherd my sheep.
The third love-question grieves Peter and restores him into feeding.
Peter’s future will move from self-direction into surrendered witness.
His death will glorify the Divine; the call remains, follow me.
Peter turns toward the beloved disciple’s path.
Peter asks about the other disciple.
Jesus returns him to his own call: you follow me.
A rumor about the beloved disciple is corrected by Jesus’ actual word.
The beloved disciple bears true witness and writes.
The works of Jesus overflow the world’s capacity for scrolls.
